prompt: Fix the lexer that doesn't handle multi-character operators

turns.content:
<task>
Fix the lexer — it tokenizes '==' as two separate '=' tokens and '>=' as '>' followed by '='. Multi-character operators should be single tokens.
</task>

arguments.summary: Read src/frontend/lexer.rs (45 lines). Token enum has single-char operators only (Eq, Lt, Gt). tokenize() matches chars one at a time. Need to add EqEq, LtEq, GtEq, NotEq tokens and check for two-char sequences.

next_steps.description: Add EqEq, LtEq, GtEq, NotEq to Token enum and add lookahead in tokenize()

arguments.content:
#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Clone)]
pub enum Token {
    Number(f64),
    Plus,
    Minus,
    Star,
    Slash,
    Eq,
    EqEq,
    NotEq,
    Lt,
    LtEq,
    Gt,
    GtEq,
    LParen,
    RParen,
    Eof,
}

turns.content:
running 5 tests
test frontend::lexer::tests::test_numbers ... ok
test frontend::lexer::tests::test_single_char_ops ... ok
test frontend::lexer::tests::test_multi_char_ops ... ok
test frontend::lexer::tests::test_mixed ... ok
test frontend::lexer::tests::test_not_eq ... ok

test result: ok. 5 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

arguments.summary: Fixed lexer in src/frontend/lexer.rs to handle multi-char operators. Added EqEq, NotEq, LtEq, GtEq tokens. Added lookahead in tokenize() — checks next char for '==', '!=', '<=', '>='. Single-char fallback preserved. All 5 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Fixed lexer to handle multi-character operators (==, !=, <=, >=) with lookahead parsing. Added 4 new token variants. All 5 tests pass.
